business.headline: Sensex ends 753 points higher, Nifty climbs over 24,500; HUL up 4%The S&P BSE Sensex jumped 753.03 points to close at 79,273.33, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 211.75 points to end at 24,576.60.

Can you skip notice period after quitting your job? What the law really saysFor many employees eager to move on to their new job, one question keeps coming up: do you really have to serve those 30, 60 or 90 days, or can you leave sooner?

Groww shares hit 52-week high: What's driving the rally and should you buy now?Groww stock up 10%: The main trigger behind the rally is strong earnings performance. The company, operated by Billionbrains Garage Ventures, reported a 122% jump in net profit to Rs 686 crore in the March quarter.

As global crude oil crisis deepens, the only cure may be a demand shockReopening the Strait of Hormuz may gradually ease supply pressures, but the world may still need lower fuel demand to stop the crisis from spilling into prices, inflation and growth. Read on to know why demand destruction may be needed to restore balance between oil supply and demand.

Should you buy a house for steady rent or invest in a shop or office for higher returns? The answer is not straightforward, because both options behave very differently in terms of income, risk and effort.

Why 71% of consumers believe loyalty rewards don't actually foster loyaltyLoyalty has always been about more than transactions – it is about the feeling of being valued. Customers stay not because of points but because a brand makes them feel recognised in ways that matter.

ICICI Bank vs HDFC Bank after Q4: Which stock looks like a better buy now?ICICI Bank gained after Q4 results while HDFC Bank slipped, raising a key investor question. Which private banking giant now offers better growth, dividends and value potential? Read on to find out.

entertainment.headline: Sydney Sweeney's cameo cut from The Devil Wears Prada 2. Here's why Sydney Sweeney's cameo in The Devil Wears Prada 2 has been cut from the final film, despite being shot. The sequel, starring Anne Hathaway and Meryl Streep, is set to release in theatres on May 1.

Odisha's K-pop icon gets a mention in South Korean President's speech. Watch President Lee Jae-Myung of South Korea praised the remarkable journey of Sriya Lenka, an Odisha girl who became India's first K-pop star, during his visit to India.

After a steady box office run and strong family appeal, the makers are exploring what comes next for the Akshay Kumar-starrer.

As lip-sync allegations gain traction, fans are divided over what really went down at her Ahmedabad show.

The unveiling extended across key sacred sites including Raman Reti Temple, Radha Rani Temple, Prem Mandir, and ISKCON Vrindavan.

Kanye West's India concert confirmed. Check date, venue and other details Global hip-hop icon Ye, formerly Kanye West, has officially announced his debut concert in India. Set for May 2026, the show marks a significant milestone in India's music scene.

Dhanush to direct a female-led film with Mrunal Thakur amid rumours? What we know Mrunal Thakur is reportedly set to feature in a female-led film, with actor Dhanush expected to share screen space with her and direct the film amid ongoing dating rumours.

general news.headline: Ahead of Pak talks, Trump's praise for Iran and a warningDonald Trump ruled out any extension to the ceasefire deadline, which will end on April 22, adding simply, "I don't want to do that. We don't have that much time."

Bengaluru woman ties lover to car to 'propose in western style', burns him aliveAccording to police, Prema told Kiran she would propose to him in a "western style," where one partner gets down on one knee and expresses their love. She then tied his hands and legs with a rope and blindfolded him.

Indian man who lost wife, daughter in Air India crash faces deportation from UKAn Indian man who lost his wife and daughter in the 2025 Ahmedabad Air India crash has been told to leave the UK by Wednesday or face deportation. Mohammad Shethwala had been living in Britain on a dependent visa and the Home Office has now rejected his plea to extend his stay on compassionate grounds.

19 of the world's 20 hottest cities today are in India. Is yours one?The weather office has said heatwave conditions are likely to persist from April 22 to April 24, with maximum temperatures expected to rise further and reach up to 43 degrees Celsius.

Bikes banned at night, no pillion riding in day: Mandate in Bengal before pollsThe Election Commission has banned bike rallies, restricted pillion riding, and prohibited two-wheeler movement at night in West Bengal.

Mallikarjun Kharge calls PM a terrorist, then clarifies. BJP gives no quarterCongress chief Mallikarjun Kharge sparked a row after calling Prime Minister Narendra Modi a "terrorist" while attacking the AIADMK for its alliance with the BJP ahead of the April 23 Tamil Nadu election. Facing backlash from the BJP, Kharge later clarified he meant PM Modi "terrorises" his opponents.

BJP's Anurag Thakur relishes maach bhaat during Bengal campaign, AAP takes swipeOn the final day of campaigning for the first phase of the West Bengal Assembly polls, BJP MP Anurag Thakur was seen enjoying maach bhaat, (fish and rice), a move that quickly drew criticism from AAP, which alleged it was being done for electoral gains.

MBA graduate ditches Rs 25 LPA job to become taxi driver, earns Rs 6.5 lakh monthlyAn MBA graduate from a Tier-2 college left behind a Rs 25 lakh job offer to become a taxi driver. Three years later, his journey took an unexpected turn.

US-Iran ceasefire ends tomorrow: Will Trump extend deadline or unleash bombs?Pakistan is hoping to host a second round of peace talks between the US and Iran tomorrow, but Tehran has yet to signal any willingness to participate. As the ceasefire deadline approaches, tensions are rising once again. With the clock ticking, the question now is: will there be a deal, a last-minute extension from Donald Trump, or a return to full-scale war?

90% women enter men's room for politics: Pappu Yadav's sexist remark sparks rowPappu Yadav's comment came against the backdrop of the women's reservation bill failing to secure a two-thirds majority in the Lok Sabha. With 298 votes in favour and 230 against, the bill fell short of the 352 votes required to amend the Constitution.

Live: Will Iran attend peace talks? Pak minister says confirmation awaitedUS-Iran-Israel War Live Updates: Iran refused to hold the second round of the ceasefire talks with the United States, citing Washington's "excessive demands, unrealistic expectations, constant shifts in stance, repeated contradictions, and the naval blockade of the maritime chokepoint, Strait of Hormuz. Meanwhile, Donald Trump said that the US delegation team, led by Vice President JD Vance, will arrive in Islamabad today. However, after Iran's refusal, further ceasefire talks regarding the prevailing West Asia conflict look bleak. Amid the developments, Pakistan's Prime Minister, Shehbaz Sharif, held a phone call with Iran's President, Masoud Pezeshkian, and discussed the regional situation. He also briefed Pezeshkian on the conversation the South Asian country had with Saudi Arabia, Qatar, and Turkey. Catch all the live updates at Indiatoday.in.
When India built a satellite inside a church, and Isro was bornThe bishop's residence was converted into offices, and nearby coconut groves turned into makeshift workshops.

Cars to run on alcohol in India? Draft rule for 85% ethanol in petrol likely soonThe government is likely to issue draft rules for E85, a fuel blend containing up to 85% ethanol and just 15% petrol. E85 could mark India's boldest fuel transition yet, turning sugarcane and grain into energy sources.

Why dry humid air is more dangerous than heatwaves in IndiaScientists have made it clear that tracking temperature alone is no longer enough. For India, a country that is humid, coastal, and monsoon-drenched, the deadliest heat often doesn't look deadly at all.

Fires at refineries in India, four other countries. Global pattern amid Iran war?A fire at a refinery in Rajasthan, just a day before its inauguration, has delayed its operations. In just 45 days since the Iran war, there have been reports of fire at oil installations in five countries, including the US and Australia. Is there a pattern to the fires amid a war in which energy has been weaponised?

Muslim BJP leader's Lenskart tilak drama flags India's short fuse problemThe incident at a Lenskart store in Mumbai, where a Muslim BJP leader barged in, intimidated the Muslim manager, and applied a tilak on staff, cannot be brushed off as just another episode in the ongoing controversy. It was a stark example of how quickly public anger in India can escalate.

He nevertheless praised Cook as "an incredible guy" and credited his leadership, while invoking co-founder Steve Jobs' legacy.

Alcaraz in race for French Open fitness, set for medical test after injuryCarlos Alcaraz faces a race against time to defend his French Open title after a wrist injury. Crucial medical tests in the coming days will determine whether he can compete at Roland-Garros next month.
